title: arthrochilium royleanum
intro:
Arthrochilium royleanum, also known as the Himalayan orchid, is a type of orchid found in the Himalayan region, characterized by its unique arthrochalus (or arthrochilus) structure, which is a modified petal that functions as a pollinia-bearing structure.

This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Arthrochilium royleanum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
